programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of Sidney Lumet, who was Oscar-nominated for his direction of 12 Angry Men, Dog Day Afternoon, Network and The Verdict.

programme.title: Last Call: When a Serial Killer Stalked Queer New York
programme.desc: The case of a serial killer targeting gay men during the Aids crisis, infiltrating New York's gay bars in search of victims.

programme.title: Rolling Stone: Stories from the Edge
programme.desc: Exploring the unique relationship between John Lennon and the magazine, and how coverage of president Richard Nixon's re-election campaign disrupted political journalism.

programme.title: Lockerbie
programme.desc: Investigators uncover a key piece of forensic evidence in the form of a fragment of a Toshiba radio that points to an Iran-backed Palestinian terror cell.

programme.title: Bobby
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating Bobby Moore, exploring his football career, including leading the England team to victory in the 1966 world cup, and the personal challenges he faced both on the pitch and in retirement. Featuring archive footage and contributions from family, friends and celebrity fans.

programme.title: Terry Venables: A Man Can Dream
programme.desc: A look at the life and times of the football player-turned-manager, who died in November 2023, intertwined with the story of England's unforgettable run at Euro 96, with insight from players, friends and family.

programme.title: Gascoigne
programme.desc: Documentary about Paul Gascoigne, which charts his rise to fame from his humble roots in the North East to the world stage and his subsequent personal problems. The film features an extensive interview with the troubled former footballing superstar, as well as comment from those who admired his skills, including Wayne Rooney, Jose Mourinho and Gazza's ex-England and Tottenham Hotspur team-mate Gar

programme.title: My Icon: Thierry Henry
programme.desc: The former Arsenal and France forward speaks about the individuals that have been an inspiration to him over the course of his career.

programme.title: UFO
programme.desc: Documentary exploring people's fascination with unidentified flying objects and the clandestine influence that the American government, private companies and the military may have in shielding the truth.

programme.title: Tell Them You Love Me
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the story of esteemed university professor Anna Stubblefield's controversial affair with Derrick Johnson, a non-verbal man with cerebral palsy.

programme.title: Six Silent Killings: Ireland's Vanishing Triangle
programme.desc: Part one of two. A look at the investigations prompted by the disappearances from a small area near Dublin of six women within the space of five years in the 1990s.

programme.title: Six Silent Killings: Ireland's Vanishing Triangle
programme.desc: Concluding the look at the investigations into the disappearances of six women within the space of five years from a small area near Dublin in the 1990s.

programme.title: The Essex Murders
programme.desc: Documentary about the killing of three notorious criminals known as the Essex Boys, who were found shot to death in December 1995. Friends and fellow gangsters reveal how the gang and their leader Tony Tucker managed to build a drug empire as the rave scene took off.

programme.title: The Essex Murders
programme.desc: A look at whether Michael Steele and Jack Whomes were really guilty of the Essex Boys murders, re-examining the questions that remain about their convictions.

programme.title: The Essex Murders
programme.desc: A look at the hunt for an alternative suspect in the Essex Boys murders, uncovering explosive revelations that could turn the case on its head.

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth
programme.desc: Documentary charting the natural world's most awe-inspiring seasonal events, including migration, rebirth and transformation, over the course of a turbulent year. At the start of the year, Arctic winds plunge the polar bear into a deep freeze, while in Australia, the heat offers a feast for one bird of prey.

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife
programme.desc: The team must take special care when they encounter the last animal of a species. Other animals to receive Hope's help are a porcupine, a bat and a seal.

programme.title: Wild Sky
programme.desc: Cameras capture footage of polar bears hunting under the aurora, track a polar vortex and follow a prairie dog family dodging tornadoes.

programme.title: Wild Latin America
programme.desc: Tensions rise as breeding season nears for the squirrel monkeys. With enemies closing in, dominant female Alba must find her alpha male suitor.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: The team races to a remote Indonesian village after they receive an anonymous tip off about a baby orangutan in need of rescue. Meanwhile on the pre-release island, some of the older orangutans practice independent living.

programme.title: Arctic Secrets
programme.desc: Bogs such as the Hudson Bay lowlands were once considered barren wastelands, but scientists now realise their importance in protecting the environment.

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife
programme.desc: A seal has a mysterious illness, Kayla finds a surprise in an attic full of baby raccoons and a former volunteer makes her debut as a veterinarian.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The female capuchins fight, Tia needs surgery, and orangutan Amy enters the late stages of pregnancy. Elsewhere, there is a new addition to the play area in the chimp nursery, and Alison visits Paulo - the baby woolly monkey she has successfully hand-reared - for the final time.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Animal director Jeremy is concerned about Ben the chimp, who has a nasty foot wound, and there is an update on Paddy and his baby Bart. Gibbon Tien undergoes a transformation and keeper Holly checks up on woolly monkey Xuzy.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Albino orangutan Alba is released back into the wild, and one of the oldest orangutans in the centre is rushed into surgery with a life-threatening condition. Meanwhile, youngsters Beni and Lala finally return to class after an incident that left them with broken limbs, and Dilla unexpectedly gives birth during the night, but the delivery does not go to plan.

programme.title: Arctic Secrets
programme.desc: The Yukon River, in which thousands of salmon make the perilous trip to reach their spawning beds, dodging predators along the way.

programme.title: Arabian Inferno
programme.desc: While mammals seem to rule over large parts of the planet, it is an entirely different story in Arabia. This is a land where the reptile is king.

programme.title: Africa's Hunters
programme.desc: A leopard that has recently become the dominant predator of an area in Zambia's Luangwa Valley must now defend its hard-won territory.

programme.title: Wild Battlegrounds
programme.desc: The challenges that animals face when trying to survive in some of the driest, fieriest places on the planet, battling exhaustion and dehydration.

programme.title: Wild Science
programme.desc: Tracing the brown bear's evolution from Ice Age giants to modern omnivores, and witnessing their bond with salmon and the intricate ecosystems that shape their survival.

programme.title: Islands
programme.desc: An exploration of the wildlife on Baffin in the Canadian Arctic, encountering arctic foxes, white beluga whales, a never-before-filmed two-tusked narwhal and more.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A growing family needs more space at their camp on Pocasset Lake; while the crew handles the remodeling, layout revamp, wall replacement and dormer addition, Ashley and Ryan come up with a way to display the family's tradition of collecting rocks.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A family finds their camp flooded when they return after winter; as the team pull back what remains of the floor, problems compound as they find powder post beetles, dry rot and more.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Builder Chase Morrill, along with Ashley Morrill-Eldridge and Ryan Eldridge, his sister and brother-in-law, transform cabins in some of the most beautiful areas of Maine. From historic cottages to camp cabins, scenic retreats get the facelifts they need.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A group of brothers who inherited a rugged hunting cabin from their father and his friends enlist the team to restore it and preserve its legacy.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and his team renovate a 130-year-old cabin located on an island; with a budget of $40,000 and a 6-week timeframe, Chase must overcome the challenges of building on an island to make sure the cabin is around for another 100 years.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders save a cabin with incredible stone work, and the guys learn pioneer stone techniques from a local expert; Mark visits two incredible restorations and Alex makes a barnwood wedding gift.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark challenges the two youngest members of the team to take down a few logs; Johnny gets a special visit from his dad; Graham reveals some secrets about the pioneers; the guys take a tour of an old grist mill; Johnny Jett's favorite covered bridge.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara seeks to transform a multifamily 1892 brick Craftsman into a single-family dwelling while keeping its original details; she and her father attempt to recover a vintage pocket door trapped inside a wall and update the home's electricity.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara continues working on an 1892 Craftsman and struggles to add a master bathroom; then, she finds a way to reuse floor boards as a porch swing, but her plan to reuse a claw-foot bathtub hits a snag because of a narrow doorway.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara Day works on restoring a 1909 Craftsman home, opening up its meandering layout and ripping out the tacky additions that have stripped the house of its original charm; she saves vintage light switches and a wallpaper mural.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara continues renovating a 1909 Craftsman home, transforming a small bedroom into the master bath; she refurbishes French doors, vintage sinks and a tub; during the walk-through, Tamara discovers the creepiest thing she's ever found in a home.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara annexes an oversized dining room to enlarge a tiny kitchen in a 1926 English Tudor; she rips out a tacky mantel to create a stunning fireplace surround; using a piece of reclaimed walnut, she creates a large desktop for an in-home office.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara Day struggles to create a more spacious master suite until deciding to add a second deck to a 1926 English Tudor; Tamara strips off numerous coats of paint to restore original doors; she adds an outdoor living area with a fire pit.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: While renovating a single-story ranch, Tamara transforms its master bedroom into a stunning, open kitchen; however, she and her father run into problems during demolition.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara continues renovations on a one-story ranch, creating a master suite; to enlarge the bath, she tears out a wall and moves the door; while polishing the copper fireplace hood, Tamara and her father use a familiar solution with unusual results.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: A couple wants to find a unique house with several acres of land, a big kitchen and space for entertaining.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: Chip and Jo meet up with a friend who hopes to buy a modern-industrial-style loft with a guest bedroom and an office in downtown Waco; after finding the perfect spot, Chip and Jo get to work tackling their first downtown loft apartment.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: A couple that loves the charm of small-scale living wants to find a place with the character of an older home.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: A wounded veteran and his girlfriend call on Chip and Jo to help them convert their house in Waco into a dreamy farmhouse; while the land is beautiful and majestic, the house is small and built inside an existing barn.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: After living in five homes in six years, a couple is ready to settle down in the Hewitt area of Texas.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: When this couple first emigrated from China, owning a beach house was the furthest thing from their minds. But decades later they're ready to join forces with their daughter and her husband to find the perfect property to renovate into their dream home.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A family searches for a beach retreat in Gulf Shores, Ala., but have different opinions on what type of house they should get.
